behavior_summary: U8P_TROWEL::look: When the player examines the trowel, the script displays a short label/description ('trowel').

U8P_TROWEL::use: When the player uses the trowel the script prompts for/obtains a target (suspend/process target). If no target is chosen it aborts. It checks reachability via Item::canReach using reach mask 0x0080 and barks 'can't reach' if unreachable.

If the chosen target's type equals 0x03 or 0x04 (inferred to represent diggable soil/ground), the script reads the target's coordinates (X, Y, Z) and creates one item (id 0x018B) at that location using FREE::createItemsAtLocation. It then sets the created item's quantity to 1, ORs status bit 0x0080 on the item (likely marking ownership/state/reachability), and calls Item::fall to finalize placement.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TROWEL::use begins by suspending to obtain a process target (assigned to local2).
Calls Item::canReach with mask 0x0080 and the selected target; on false it barks 'can't reach' and returns.
On reachable target it queries Item::getX, Item::getY, Item::getZ and Item::getType and branches: only proceed to create items when type == 0x03 or type == 0x04.
After creation the sequence is: FREE::createItemsAtLocation -> Item::setQuantity(1) -> Item::orStatus(0x0080) -> Item::fall, then return.
open_questions: What concrete world objects/types do target type codes 0x03 and 0x04 represent (likely different ground/soil types but not confirmed)?
What item does id 0x018B correspond to in-game (what the trowel produces)?
What is the semantic meaning of the 0x0080 status bit when applied via Item::orStatus (ownership, reachability, or another marker)?
Why is local2 reused (first as target id, later overwritten with Z in decompilation) — is that an artifact of compiler temporaries or intentional reuse?

main_function.name: U8P_TROWEL::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examination label/description for the trowel when the player uses the Look/Examine action.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_TROWEL::look/examine process for the TROWEL object (player inspects the item).

function_map.name: U8P_TROWEL::use
function_map.role: handle item U8P_TROWEL::use: select target, guard reachability, create/place resulting item at target

external_calls.purpose: Prompt the player to choose a target or obtain the process target; returns selected target id assigned to local2.
Check whether the selected target is reachable by the actor using reach mask 0x0080; used to guard action and trigger 'can't reach' bark if false.
Read target's X coordinate or location descriptor for placement.
Read target's Y coordinate for placement.
Read target's Z/floor value (decompilation shows value stored into local2 in one place).
Obtain the target's type code (checked against 0x03 and 0x04 to decide whether to create/place the item).
Create the resulting item(s) at the chosen location (observed count=1, item id=0x018B); returns created-item id stored in a local.
Set the created item's quantity to 1.
Apply status bits (OR with 0x0080) to the created item, likely marking ownership/state/reachability.
Finalize placement so the created item 'falls' into the world at the target location.
Display the message 'can't reach' when Item::canReach returns false.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_TROWEL::look: When the player examines the trowel, the script displays a short label/description ('trowel').
